eventMetadata.context_description: Oklahoma City Thunder, leading the Western Conference at 62-16 and one win from clinching the #1 seed, hold a depth and form edge heading into tonight's road matchup at the Clippers' Intuit Dome. Jalen Williams returns from hamstring injury management after OKC's dominant 123-87 rout of the Lakers yesterday, with only rookie Thomas Sorber sidelined long-term by a torn ACL. The Clippers, battling for a top-8 playoff spot around 46-32, welcome back Kawhi Leonard on the second night of a back-to-back but remain shorthanded without Darius Garland (toe), Isaiah Jackson (ankle), and others like Bradley Beal (hip). Thunder's elite defense (106.3 points allowed per game) and recent momentum contrast Clippers' home-court advantage and late-season urgency.

title: Will the U.S. invade Iran before 2027?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States commences a military offensive intended to establish control over any portion of Iran by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, land de facto controlled by Iran or the United States as of November 4, 2025 12:00 PM ET, will be considered the sovereign territory of that country.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible sources.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects a 68.5% implied probability against a U.S. ground invasion of Iran before 2027, driven by recent de-escalation signals amid high risks of prolonged conflict. Following U.S.-Israeli airstrikes that killed Supreme Leader Khamenei on March 1, 2026, and subsequent operations sinking parts of Iran's navy, President Trump announced a two-week ceasefire last week, paving the way for negotiations on a 10-point Iranian peace plan. Diplomatic progress, including signs of Strait of Hormuz reopening and falling oil prices, has tempered escalation fears, while Iran's fortified terrain, proxy forces like Hezbollah and Houthis, and potential for massive U.S. casualties deter boots-on-the-ground commitments. Public polls show low support for deeper involvement, with focus on targeted strikes and UAE attacks on Iranian oil facilities. Upcoming Vienna talks could further reduce invasion risks.

title: Will the Iranian regime fall by April 30?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Islamic Republic of Iran’s current ruling regime is overthrown, collapsed, or otherwise ceases to govern by April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This requires a broad consensus of reporting indicating that core structures of the Islamic Republic (e.g. the office of the Supreme Leader, the Guardian Council, IRGC control under clerical authority) have been dissolved, incapacitated, or replaced by a fundamentally different governing system or otherwise lost de facto power over a majority of the population of Iran. This could occur via revolution, civil war, military coup, or voluntary abdication, but only qualifies if the Islamic Republic no longer exercises sovereign power.

Routine political events such as elections, reforms, or leadership succession do not qualify. Internal coups or power shifts that preserve the Islamic Republic’s core structures also do not qualify. Only a clear break in continuity—such as a new provisional government, revolutionary council, or constitution replacing the Islamic Republic will qualify.

Partial loss of territory or challenges from rebel or exile groups will not qualify unless the Islamic Republic no longer administers the majority of the Iranian population within Iran. 

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices a 97.1% implied probability against the Iranian regime falling by April 30, driven by its demonstrated resilience following U.S.-Israel airstrikes that killed Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei in March, amid earlier economic-triggered protests suppressed without security force defections. The regime, now under successor leadership, maintained control via loyal IRGC and Basij forces, with opposition lacking organized ground presence. Yesterday's U.S.-brokered two-week ceasefire—exchanging Strait of Hormuz access for negotiations on Iran's 10-point plan—further stabilizes the situation, reducing escalation risks in the narrow three-week window. Realistic shifts would require ceasefire collapse, mass IRGC mutiny, or sudden nationwide uprising, all deemed improbable by traders given historical patterns of regime survival.

eventMetadata.context_description: Barcelona top the La Liga table with 73 points from 29 matches, seven points clear of Real Madrid after a dominant run including a 3-0 Copa del Rey win over Atlético Madrid on March 3—though Los Colchoneros advanced on aggregate to the final. Heading into the April 4 clash at the Metropolitano, trader consensus hinges on Barça's attacking firepower led by Lamine Yamal and Pedri amid Raphinha's fresh right hamstring injury from Brazil duty, potentially joining doubts over full-backs Koundé and Balde. Atlético, fourth in standings, face absences like goalkeeper Jan Oblak (muscle strain) and midfielder Pablo Barrios (thigh), but boast home strength under Simeone and momentum from a recent Madrid derby loss. Recent form favors Barça's away resilience, while Atlético's defensive setup could exploit any visitor fatigue.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the rubber match of their three-game series at PNC Park, the San Diego Padres (6-6) face the Pittsburgh Pirates (7-5) with Michael King (0-1, 3.38 ERA) starting against Mitch Keller (1-0, 1.50 ERA), following a 1-1 split—Padres' 5-0 shutout in Game 1 via Germán Márquez and Pirates' 7-1 rout behind Paul Skenes' near no-hitter in Game 2. Pittsburgh holds home-field edge and boasts a stronger early-season record, but San Diego's offense features hot bats like Jackson Merrill and Nick Castellanos amid bullpen strains from injuries (Padres' Yuki Matsui groin IL, Griffin Canning Achilles IL; Pirates' Jared Jones 60-day IL). Both rotations rank mid-pack, with weather-neutral conditions favoring a low-scoring duel shaped by starter efficiency and late-inning relief.

title: Eurovision Winner 2026
description:
This market will resolve to the country whose candidate for Eurovision 2026 wins.

If at any point it is impossible for the listed candidate to win Eurovision 2026 based on the rules of the competition (i.e. they are eliminated), this market may immediately resolve to "No".

If no winner is announced by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve "Other". All ties will be broken according to EBU's official Eurovision rules.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Eurovision (https://eurovision.tv/), including live footage of Eurovision 2026, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket crowns Finland as the frontrunner at 39% implied probability to win Eurovision 2026 in Vienna, propelled by Linda Lampenius and Pete Parkkonen's commanding UMK victory in late February with "Liekinheitin"—a genre-blending track fusing violin virtuosity, rock energy, and dance hooks that dominated national polls and early fan votes like OGAE. France (10.9%) and Denmark (10.2%) trail closely, bolstered by internal selections and jury-friendly entries—"Eclipse" by Delta Goodrem for Australia (6.3%) and Akylas's "Ferto" for Greece (5.9%) also gain traction amid surging streaming buzz. Recent semi-final running order reveals and full song releases have sharpened momentum, though non-Big 5 qualifiers like Finland face qualification hurdles before the May 12-16 showdown, where televote surges or jury upsets could shift the narrative.
